framework.name: Next.js
framework.reason: Standard NEXOS — SSR/SSG, React Server Components, streaming, native ViewTransitions API support for page transitions

language.name: TypeScript
language.reason: Standard NEXOS — maximum type safety, critical for auto-documentation pipeline that extracts props from interfaces

css.name: Tailwind CSS
css.reason: Standard NEXOS — CSS-first config in v4 aligns with dynamic theming via CSS custom properties, native @theme tokens

i18n.name: next-intl
i18n.reason: Standard NEXOS — App Router native integration, type-safe message keys, middleware-based locale detection

testing.reason: Standard NEXOS — Vitest ESM-native, fast HMR, compatible with React 19 + Server Components testing

linting.reason: Standard NEXOS — Next.js conventions + accessibility linting mandatory

images.name: next/image
images.reason: Standard NEXOS — automatic optimization, lazy loading, AVIF/WebP, responsive sizes

fonts.name: next/font
fonts.reason: Standard NEXOS — self-hosted, zero layout shift, fonts validated in Ph0 benchmark

deployment.reason: Standard NEXOS — native Next.js CI/CD, edge middleware for CSP nonce + i18n + theme cookie

optional_additions.name: Framer Motion
optional_additions.reason: Experiment transitions, shared layout animations (layoutId), scroll-triggered reveals, showroom presentation mode
optional_additions.condition: Brief mandates animations: hover lift, fade-up stagger, layout transitions between experiment views

optional_additions.name: @ai-sdk/openai + ai (Vercel AI SDK)
optional_additions.reason: Agent protocol for 3 internal agents (DevOps, UI-Generator, Business-Strategist) with streaming responses
optional_additions.condition: Brief defines 3 AI agents as core feature; Vercel AI SDK is the designated agent protocol

optional_additions.name: @tiptap/react + extensions
optional_additions.reason: Rich-text notes editor with slash commands, inline toolbar, code blocks with syntax highlighting
optional_additions.condition: Brief specifies Tiptap as notes editor engine; no viable lighter alternative for slash-command + block-based editing

optional_additions.name: @next/mdx + next-mdx-remote
optional_additions.reason: MDX rendering for notes content, auto-generated experiment documentation, and legal page templates
optional_additions.condition: Brief specifies MDX as notes format; auto-documentation pipeline outputs MDX

optional_additions.name: Radix UI Primitives
optional_additions.reason: Unstyled, accessible primitives for dialog, dropdown, tabs, tooltip — handles focus management, keyboard navigation, ARIA automatically
optional_additions.condition: 4 themes require unstyled base components; Radix is the industry standard (shadcn, v0 both use it)

optional_additions.name: Storybook
optional_additions.reason: Component documentation, visual regression testing, experiment showcase with per-theme previews
optional_additions.condition: Brief mandates Storybook integration; auto-documentation pipeline feeds into Storybook docs addon

optional_additions.name: Zod
optional_additions.reason: Runtime schema validation for agent inputs/outputs, experiment metadata, form submissions, API route payloads
optional_additions.condition: 3 agents produce unstructured AI outputs requiring runtime validation; form validation with react-hook-form

optional_additions.name: React Hook Form
optional_additions.reason: Performant uncontrolled form management for settings, contact form, experiment metadata editing
optional_additions.condition: Multiple forms: contact, settings, experiment metadata, agent configuration. Zod resolver for schema-driven validation

optional_additions.name: next-themes
optional_additions.reason: Theme attribute management on <html>, cookie-based persistence, FOWT prevention, SSR-safe hydration
optional_additions.condition: 4 dynamic themes with data-theme attribute switching; needs cookie persistence via middleware for FOWT prevention

explicitly_rejected.name: jQuery
explicitly_rejected.reason: Obsolete, incompatible with React 19 virtual DOM, +90KB uncompressed, zero value in modern stack

explicitly_rejected.name: styled-components
explicitly_rejected.reason: Runtime CSS-in-JS — performance penalty on SSR hydration, incompatible with React Server Components streaming, conflicts with Tailwind utility-first approach

explicitly_rejected.name: Pages Router
explicitly_rejected.reason: Legacy Next.js routing — App Router is the standard since Next.js 13, required for Server Components, streaming SSR, and parallel routes

explicitly_rejected.name: CSS-in-JS runtime (Emotion, Stitches)
explicitly_rejected.reason: Runtime style injection breaks streaming SSR, conflicts with Tailwind CSS, adds unnecessary bundle weight. Tailwind + CSS custom properties covers all theming needs

explicitly_rejected.name: Chakra UI
explicitly_rejected.reason: Runtime CSS-in-JS under the hood (Emotion), opinionated design system conflicts with 4-theme architecture, heavy bundle (+100KB), poor RSC compatibility

explicitly_rejected.name: MUI (Material UI)
explicitly_rejected.reason: Emotion-based runtime CSS-in-JS, Material Design opinions conflict with Bento/Glassmorphism/Cyberpunk themes, +200KB bundle, poor tree-shaking

explicitly_rejected.name: GSAP
explicitly_rejected.reason: Overkill for UI transitions, proprietary license for commercial use, Framer Motion covers all required animation patterns with better React integration

explicitly_rejected.name: Prisma / Drizzle / any ORM
explicitly_rejected.reason: No database in Phase 1 scope — notes stored as local MDX files, experiment metadata as JSON. Database layer deferred to future phase if needed

explicitly_rejected.name: Redux / Zustand
explicitly_rejected.reason: No global client state management needed — React Server Components + URL state + React context covers all state patterns. Agent chat state is local to chat panels

explicitly_rejected.name: tRPC
explicitly_rejected.reason: No separate backend — Next.js API routes with Zod validation provide type-safe API layer without additional abstraction

csp_strategy.description: Middleware generates a cryptographic nonce per request, injected into CSP header and passed to Script/style tags. Allows inline scripts for next-themes hydration while blocking XSS.
csp_strategy.notes: style-src unsafe-inline required for Tailwind CSS injection in dev mode; in production, consider hash-based approach. connect-src includes AI provider endpoints for agent protocol.

adr_log.title: Framer Motion for UI animations
adr_log.context: Brief mandates hover lift, fade-up stagger, shared layout transitions between experiment views, and presentation mode animations. CSS animations alone cannot handle layout animations (layoutId), gesture-driven interactions, or orchestrated stagger sequences across dynamic lists.
adr_log.decision: Include Framer Motion v12 as the animation library. Use layout and layoutId for experiment grid-to-detail transitions. Use AnimatePresence for route transitions. Enforce prefers-reduced-motion respect globally.
adr_log.consequences: +15KB gzipped to client bundle. All animations must be wrapped in a motion config that respects prefers-reduced-motion. Tree-shake unused features via modular imports.

alternatives_considered.name: CSS animations only
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Cannot handle layout animations, gesture-driven interactions, or coordinated stagger across dynamic lists

alternatives_considered.name: GSAP
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Proprietary license for commercial use, heavier bundle, worse React integration

alternatives_considered.name: Motion One
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Lacks React-specific features (AnimatePresence, layout), smaller ecosystem

adr_log.title: Vercel AI SDK for agent protocol
adr_log.context: Platform requires 3 internal AI agents (DevOps, UI-Generator, Business-Strategist) with streaming responses, structured output, and tool calling. The brief designates Vercel AI SDK as the agent protocol.
adr_log.decision: Use Vercel AI SDK v4 (ai + @ai-sdk/openai packages). Implement agents as Next.js API route handlers with streaming. Use Zod schemas for structured output validation. Agent system prompts stored in /config/agents/ as TypeScript constants.
adr_log.consequences: Server-side only dependency for API routes (+0KB client bundle for core). Requires API keys for LLM providers (stored in environment variables, never committed). Rate limiting must be implemented per-agent to control costs.

alternatives_considered.name: LangChain
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Heavier abstraction, more complex, not specified in brief

alternatives_considered.name: Direct OpenAI SDK
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: No streaming UI primitives, no provider abstraction, more boilerplate

alternatives_considered.name: Custom fetch-based streaming
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Reinventing streaming response parsing, error handling, and abort controller management

adr_log.title: Tiptap for notes rich-text editor
adr_log.context: Notes engine requires slash commands, inline formatting toolbar, code blocks with syntax highlighting, task lists, and MDX-compatible output. The brief specifies Tiptap as the editor.
adr_log.decision: Use Tiptap v2 with modular extensions: starter-kit, placeholder, link, code-block-lowlight, task-list, task-item. Editor is client-only (use dynamic import with ssr: false). Output serialized to MDX-compatible markdown.
adr_log.consequences: +45KB gzipped client bundle for editor views. Editor must be dynamically imported to avoid SSR hydration issues. Tiptap extensions are individually selectable — only import what is needed. Autosave with debounce (300ms) to localStorage, with conflict detection for multi-tab.

alternatives_considered.name: ProseMirror (raw)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Tiptap is built on ProseMirror but provides React bindings and extension system — raw ProseMirror requires significantly more boilerplate

alternatives_considered.name: Slate.js
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Less mature extension ecosystem, harder to implement slash commands, smaller community

alternatives_considered.name: Monaco Editor
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Code editor, not rich-text editor — wrong tool for notes with mixed content

adr_log.title: MDX for notes rendering and documentation
adr_log.context: Notes engine uses MDX format for storage and rendering. Auto-documentation pipeline generates MDX from TypeScript interfaces. Legal pages are templated MDX populated from brief-client.json.
adr_log.decision: Use @next/mdx for static MDX pages (legal) and next-mdx-remote for dynamic MDX rendering (notes, auto-docs). MDX components map to themed UI primitives. Custom remark/rehype plugins for frontmatter extraction and code block enhancement.
adr_log.consequences: +8KB gzipped for MDX runtime. MDX compilation happens server-side (RSC compatible). Custom components must be registered in an MDX provider. Security: sanitize user-generated MDX to prevent XSS via component injection.

alternatives_considered.name: Plain Markdown (remark)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Cannot embed React components, limits auto-doc rendering capabilities

alternatives_considered.name: Contentlayer
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Deprecated/unmaintained, next-mdx-remote is actively maintained and more flexible

alternatives_considered.name: Markdoc (Stripe)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Different syntax from MDX, smaller ecosystem, team familiar with MDX

adr_log.title: Radix UI for accessible component primitives
adr_log.context: 4 dynamic themes (Bento, Glassmorphism, Minimalist, Cyberpunk) require unstyled base components that can be themed entirely via CSS custom properties and Tailwind classes. Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) is non-negotiable.
adr_log.decision: Use Radix UI primitives for all interactive patterns: Dialog, DropdownMenu, Tabs, Tooltip, ToggleGroup, Select, Popover, Switch, Toast, ScrollArea, Separator. Style exclusively with Tailwind classes referencing CSS custom properties from the active theme.
adr_log.consequences: +2-4KB per primitive (individually tree-shakeable, only import used primitives). All keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA attributes handled automatically. No visual opinions — fully compatible with all 4 themes.

alternatives_considered.name: Headless UI (Tailwind Labs)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Fewer primitives available, slower release cadence, less battle-tested than Radix

alternatives_considered.name: Ark UI
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Newer, smaller community, less documentation, Radix is the proven standard

alternatives_considered.name: Custom primitives
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Massive effort to implement accessible dialog, dropdown, tooltip correctly — Radix solves this definitively

adr_log.title: Storybook 8 for component documentation and experiment showcase
adr_log.context: Brief mandates Storybook integration for component documentation. Auto-documentation pipeline generates Storybook stories from experiment metadata. Showroom needs visual previews per theme.
adr_log.decision: Use Storybook 8 with Next.js framework integration. Addons: essentials (controls, actions, viewport), a11y (accessibility checks), themes (4-theme switching in Storybook), interactions (play functions for testing). Each experiment in /experiments/[cat]/[feat]/ includes a .stories.tsx file.
adr_log.consequences: Dev-only dependency — zero production bundle impact. Requires .storybook/ config directory. Build time increases for Storybook static export. Theme addon configured to mirror the 4 runtime themes.

alternatives_considered.name: Ladle
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Fewer addons, no a11y addon, smaller community

alternatives_considered.name: Histoire
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Vue-focused, React support is secondary

alternatives_considered.name: Custom docs site
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Massive effort to replicate controls, viewport switching, a11y checking that Storybook provides out of the box

adr_log.title: Zod for runtime schema validation
adr_log.context: AI agents produce unstructured outputs that need runtime validation before rendering. Experiment metadata.json files need schema validation. Form submissions need server-side validation. API route payloads need validation.
adr_log.decision: Use Zod v3 as the single runtime validation library. Define schemas in /types/ alongside TypeScript types (infer types from Zod schemas where possible). Use with React Hook Form via @hookform/resolvers. Use with Vercel AI SDK structured output.
adr_log.consequences: +3KB gzipped (tree-shakeable). Single source of truth for runtime + compile-time types via z.infer<>. All API routes validate input with Zod before processing.

alternatives_considered.name: Yup
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Larger bundle, weaker TypeScript inference, Zod is the modern standard

alternatives_considered.name: Valibot
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Smaller community, less integration with Vercel AI SDK and React Hook Form

alternatives_considered.name: TypeScript only (no runtime)
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Types are erased at runtime — AI outputs, user input, and API payloads need runtime validation

adr_log.title: React Hook Form for form management
adr_log.context: Multiple forms: contact form, settings (theme, language, agent config), experiment metadata editing, cookie consent preferences. Need performant form handling with validation.
adr_log.decision: Use React Hook Form v7 with Zod resolver. Uncontrolled inputs by default for performance. Server Actions for form submission where possible (progressive enhancement).
adr_log.consequences: +5KB gzipped. Uncontrolled inputs reduce re-renders. Zod resolver bridges runtime validation with form state. All forms work without JavaScript (progressive enhancement via Server Actions).

alternatives_considered.name: Formik
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Controlled inputs by default (more re-renders), larger bundle, less active maintenance

alternatives_considered.name: Server Actions only
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: No client-side validation feedback, poor UX for complex forms like settings

alternatives_considered.name: Native form validation
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Inconsistent browser implementations, no custom error styling, no complex validation rules

adr_log.title: next-themes for theme management
adr_log.context: 4 dynamic themes (Bento, Glassmorphism, Minimalist, Cyberpunk) need runtime switching without page reload. Theme must persist across sessions via cookie. Flash of Wrong Theme (FOWT) must be prevented on initial load.
adr_log.decision: Use next-themes v0.4 with attribute='data-theme' (not class-based). Configure with storageKey='theme', defaultTheme='minimalist'. Extend with custom cookie sync in middleware for SSR-safe theme rendering. Cyberpunk forces data-mode='dark' regardless of system preference.
adr_log.consequences: +1KB gzipped. Theme attribute set before hydration via injected script (prevents FOWT). Middleware reads theme cookie to set correct data-theme on server render. Custom logic needed for Cyberpunk dark-only enforcement.

alternatives_considered.name: Custom React context
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Must solve FOWT, cookie sync, SSR hydration mismatch — next-themes solves all of these

alternatives_considered.name: CSS media queries only
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Only supports light/dark, not 4 named themes

alternatives_considered.name: Tailwind darkMode config
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Only supports binary dark/light toggle, not multi-theme switching

bundle_budget.notes: Tiptap (~45KB) and MDX runtime (~8KB) are loaded only on /notes routes via dynamic import. Vercel AI SDK is server-only. Storybook is dev-only.
